title: Pearson Edexcel International GCSE Biology Paper 2B - January 2020
subject: Biology

sub_questions.ms_text: An explanation that makes reference to two of the following points: 1. (damage to) alveoli (1). 2. reduces / less / small surface area (1). 3. (less) diffusion / (less) oxygen in / (less) carbon dioxide out (1).

An explanation that makes reference to the following points: 1. bacteria / microorganisms / pathogens are killed / destroyed / removed (1). 2. no / less chance of infection (with TB) / disease / catching TB (1).

Any two from: 1. fewer cattle (to be infected) (1). 2. fewer people in Europe (than in rest of world) (1). 3. cattle are screened / if infected are killed (1). 4. cattle are given antibiotics (1). 5. vaccination (of cattle / people / badgers) (1). 6. more pasteurised milk drunk (1). 7. fewer badgers/animals to transmit TB (1).
11.1 : 1 (2 marks). (Calculation: 147,710 / 13,297). Allow 1 mark for 13,297 and 147,710 OR 1 mark for 1:11.1.
Any two from: 1. (find / protect / less competition for) mates / females (1). 2. (protect) food / less competition for food / resources (1). 3. protect young (1).

An explanation that makes reference to three of the following: 1. use attenuated / dead / weak / inactive pathogen (1). 2. antigens (1). 3. memory cells (1). 4. secondary immune response (1). 5. antibody production fast(er) / soon(er) / more (1).

An answer that makes reference to three of the following points: 1. vaccinate badgers in one area and not in another (1). 2. (count) number of cattle with TB (1). 3. same number / control number of badgers in each area (1). 4. same field size / area (1). 5. same number of cattle (1).

An explanation that makes reference to the following points: 1. draws water up (to leaves) (1). 2. cooling (1). 3. (transport) minerals (1). 4. prevent wilting / maintain turgidity (1). 5. (water for) photosynthesis (1).

An explanation that makes reference to the following points: 1. wind moves water away (from plant surface) (1). 2. creates / maintains / increases (concentration) gradient (1). 3. (more) (water moves out of plant by) diffusion / evaporation (1).

Factors to control (any two pairs): 1. same light intensity (1) as it affects stomata opening (1). 2. same temperature (1) as it affects diffusion / speed of molecules / energy / evaporation (1). 3. humidity (1) as it affects concentration gradient (1). 4. same species/age/size/mass/leaf area (1) as it affects number of stomata / thickness of leaves (1). 5. same CO2 concentration (1) as it affects stomata opening (1).

Correct Answer: A (the left ventricle is thick to pump blood to the body). B is incorrect because left ventricle is not thin. C is incorrect because right ventricle pumps to lungs. D is incorrect because right ventricle wall is not thick.

An explanation that makes reference to three of the following points: 1. ventricle (wall) is thinner / less muscle (1). 2. coronary artery is blocked / less blood passing through (1). 3. less oxygen / glucose (1). 4. less (aerobic) respiration / anaerobic respiration occurs / less ATP (1). 5. cell / muscle / tissue death (1).
An explanation that makes reference to: 1. (stem cells can) differentiate / can become muscle cells / unspecialised cells (1). 2. stem cells divide / mitosis (1).

An explanation that makes reference to two of: 1. cells have same DNA / genes / alleles (1). 2. same antigens / tissue type / blood group (1). 3. less risk of rejection / no immune response (1). 4. no risk of infection / disease spread (1).

Any three from: 1. DNA is double stranded / helix, RNA is single stranded (1). 2. DNA has thymine, RNA has uracil (1). 3. DNA has deoxyribose, RNA has ribose (1). 4. DNA is long(er), RNA is short(er) (1).

A description including five points: 1. transcription in nucleus (1). 2. transcription uses DNA to make (m)RNA (1). 3. DNA unzips / one strand copied (1). 4. translation at ribosomes / in cytoplasm (1). 5. translation involves mRNA and tRNA (1). 6. anticodons bind to codons (1). 7. translation produces amino acid chain / protein (1).
3 marks for correct explanation: 1. 4^3 = 64 (1). 2. 64 is greater than 20 combinations needed (1). 3. 4^2 = 16 which is not enough (1).
Correct Answer: A (collecting duct).
Correct Answer: D (T - glomerulus/Bowman's).
Correct Answer: A (P - collecting duct).
Correct Answer: B (R - proximal convoluted tubule).
1. increasing birth mass associated with more nephrons (1). 2. positive correlation / linear (1).

An explanation including four points: 1. proteins are large molecules (1). 2. normally will not pass out of glomerulus / into filtrate (1). 3. high blood pressure forces protein molecules out (1). 4. damage to glomerulus / Bowman's capsule / basement membrane (1).
1. add Biuret (1). 2. changes to purple / lilac / mauve (1).
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. mix microorganisms with food substrates / prevent settling (1). 2. mix/distribute oxygen (1). 3. keep temperature uniform (1).
1. oxygen for aerobic respiration (1). 2. remove bacteria/microorganisms to prevent competition/contamination (1).
An explanation that makes reference to two: 1. monitor/control temperature (1). 2. turn on/off cooling jacket water (1). 3. prevent enzymes being denatured / maintain optimal temperature (1).
Hh and Hh.

Any two from: 1. small sample size (1). 2. random/chance of fertilisation (1). 3. fewer yellow / more brown than predicted (1). 4. no HH offspring survive / yellow mice die / ratio is 2 Hh : 1 hh (1). 5. mutations (1).
